entry.title: Re: Deployment error in SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Unable to download Agent 3 on Mac
entry.content: After clicking download a download progress bar appears and then you accept the download. That said, it seems to me that it is a problem with your firewall, antivirus or some restriction from your organization.

entry.title: Re: Interested to host CodeJam
entry.content:
Hi @purusottam_agar ,

Let me know if you are interested to host CodeJam on SAP Integration Suite. We can plan it out.

Best Regards,

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: Customer Proposal Clarification
entry.content:
Hi CAA,

Thanks for the response.

The responses are not clear. Customer is requesting more information, as this Q's are RISE SOP related and could you please provide detailed explanation.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Deployment error in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello, 

These could be a possible reason:

 

1. there has been deletion of connectors / app during the development. 

what you can do is:
1. Save your work
2. Restart your browser and clear cache
3. On the process studio - double check the missing connectors/link. 
4. See the "End" event? - Remove it and add it back.

Double check as well if there are missing text fields or required parameters in other artifacts you are using (Forms, Automation, etc).

lastly Save -> Release -> Deploy.

 

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: SAP Code-Jam Request in Dubai
entry.content:
Hi @Harish_Vatsa ,

We are working on this and I will set up a call in Jan 1st week to discuss further.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Hybrid Scenario: Usage of SAP HANA Calculation View on SAP Datasphere

entry.title: How to automate SAP BTP Platform User management?
entry.content:
With a mindset to automate repeated tasks, I'm exploring on how we can automate User Management on SAP BTP at all current possible levels such as 

- Global Account

     - Subaccount

         - Cloud Foundry

         - Kyma

         - ABAP

I currently reviewed below API's

- https://api.sap.com/api/AuthorizationAPI

- https://api.sap.com/api/PlatformAPI

and BTP , CF CLI services.

While CLI gives us flexibility of User management, we need the client to be installed [local, cloud] to execute the tasks.

I'm seeking inputs on if we have a way to build and deploy a central user management tool on BTP that can give us a runtime to execute BTP , CF  commands thereby manage User management from a UI service through CAPM.

Best Regards,

Sunil Chintalapati

entry.title: Re: PRAT1, PRAT2 Fields are not showing at MDG UI Level u...
entry.content:
Hi Rishav,

Please check the Genil Component of the entity and check if the structure are properly generated .

If the structures are generated properly then you can add then to the UI , let me know if this helps else you can provide more details 

Regards,

Manas

entry.title: SuccessFactors People Analytics (Stories): How to create change report (current vs previous values)
entry.content:
The other day, I was asked if it was possible to create a change report in Stories, similar to what we used to do in Canvas. Although I always assumed it was possible, I never had the time to explore it until now. To my surprise, I couldn't find the equivalent of the great Advance Reporting – Create Change Reports Manually guide for People Analytics (Stories). Hence, this blog post!

Step 1:
Start by creating the query with the "Today's value." The principle is quite similar to Advance Reporting. First, you need to select an "anchor" table to initiate the query. In this case, I selected the Personal Info table and added the related Job Information table. By default, when you create a new query the Time filter of the anchor table is set to Today’s and added related table set to Same Filter as First Table, please leave this it that!

 

Step 2:
Then, Duplicate the Job Information table. It's important to note that, similar to Canvas, you cannot copy the starting ("anchor") table. The option to duplicate is only available for an added related table.

Step 3:
Modify the time filter on the Copy table ONLY to include all records. To do this, click on the table, then select the Filter icon. From there, choose the Time Filter option and select Remove filter. It's important not to modify the time filter of the original Job Info table or Personal Info, which should remain set as "Today".

Step 4:
Now, it's time to select the columns we need for our report. Let’s say the objective of this report is to identify changes in the employee's Location (a Foundation Object) and on the employee's Work Arrangement Type (a picklist).

I have selected the fields needed and re-labeled everything to make it easier to follow along for the rest of this blog. Additionally, I have also included the Start Date, End Date, and Event Reason columns. I find it helpful to have these columns for testing the accuracy of our report at the end...

Step 5:
At this point, if you Preview the Query, it won’t look good at all 😊. Don’t worry, we just need a bit of filtering to clean it up! But let’s try to understand what we see. (PS: I’m not database specialist but let’s try to explain it!).

 

  • For each employment record, the system will generate a line. This information is retrieved from the Copy table, which does not have a time filter, resulting in all employee records being included in the output. In our example above, the employee has 2 records in Job Info, hence 2 lines in our report (for now 😉).
  • Each line will consist of Today’s values for all fields labeled as "Current" on the left side. These values are obtained from the Job Information original table, which only provides values as of Today's date (as per its Time Filter). Additionally, on the right side of the line, the query returns values from each saved record (including the Today’s values). It does look weirder if you have more saved records, but basically each line is a combination of today’s record and another record of the employee.

Step 6:
Our next steps are focused on refining the query to ensure that it returns only one line for each employee. This line should include the current record for today and the corresponding previous record, which ended the day before the start date of the current record. To accomplish this, we will create calculated columns that compare the Start Date of the current record with the End Date of the previous record. If there is a one-day gap between these dates, it indicates the line we want to keep in our results. This comparison will help us filter and identify the correct records to include in our final query result. Here is the formula:

Step 7:
Now, we need to apply a filter to our query based on the calculated column we created. This filter will ensure that we keep only the line that contains the data for the current and previous records. Additionally, we will take this opportunity to include only active employees in our results.

Step 8:
We're almost there! The last query step is to create calculated columns for the fields where we want to see the changed data. These calculated columns will help us identify and display the specific changes that have occurred. By creating these columns, we can easily compare the values between the current and previous records and highlight any differences. Here is our formulas for the Location change and the Work Arrangement Change columns:

Step 9:
Now, let’s wrap things up and preview the result in a Story. Finish the query and create a beautiful (or useful) table. In this table, we will be able to see the Current and Previous Values for the selected fields, as well as verify that our Changed column is functioning correctly.

Conclusion:
Therefore, this is how we can create change columns in Stories.

I hope you found this post helpful and informative! If you enjoyed reading it and found value in the information shared, I would greatly appreciate some Kudoes in return. Your support and encouragement through virtual high-fives is part of my motivation to continue sharing cool things to do in SuccessFactors.

Cheers,
Stéphanie

entry.title: Beginner’s Learning Venture with Gen AI to Create a Chat Assistant
entry.content:
No doubt Gen AI term has grabbed the attention of the whole world. And everyone now wants to try out its potential. Chat GPT, Google Gemini has become everyone’s personal assistant to get things drafted, to get ideas, to get potential solutions over technical error etc.

It has become a topic of discussion in every company and as a goal for individuals for next review meet 😉

But well, it’s interesting to know, how do these things work out.

Tried out our own SAP AI Core to get a hands-on by creating a chat assistant.

Purpose: To use ‘generative-ai-hub-sdk’ to access the ‘gpt-35-turbo’ LLM model from SAP's Generative AI Hub in AI Core

 

Few Pre-requisite Steps Required:

  • Provision SAP AI core entitlement in SAP BTP cockpit and create the corresponding instance with sap-internal service plan and generate the service key.
  • The service key details enable to derive the authentication by using the clientid, clientsecret and request auth_url to generate Auth Token. We saved the following values as .env file with below details.
  • Next Create the deployment configuration using the below curl command by providing the executableId, modelName and modelVersion
  • The response of the same would provide configurationid, which needs to be passed to start the deployment, which would in turn provide deploymentId

  • Once the desired LLM is deployed that will help to understand the text-based context, as its built using deep learning techniques, enabling to capture complex patterns in language based on various parameters.

     

     

    Next went ahead with chat completion using the LangChain PromptTemplates. Also tried with memory using langchain.memory  ConversationBufferWindowMemory.

  • ChatPromptTemplate helps to structure and format prompts to design multi turn interaction.

    • SystemMessagePromptTemplate sets the initial context or behaviour for the AI.
    • HumanMessagePromptTemplate represents the user's input.
    • AIMessagePromptTemplate is used to generate the AI's response.

     

     

    ‘temperature’, ‘max_tokens’, and ‘top_p’ are parameters in a model that determines the behaviour or helps to fine tune the output.

     

    • A higher temperature (e.g., 0.7 to 1.0) makes the output more random and creative, while a lower temperature (e.g., 0.0 to 0.3) makes the output more focused and deterministic.

     

    • max_tokens determine the maximum number of tokens (words or word pieces) that the model can generate in the response.

     

    • top_p controls the cumulative probability for nucleus sampling.

     

    Output:

         May be a small start for people who are still yet to venture out…..

 

 

Learning References:

Learning how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Techn

Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad | SAP Tutorials

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: It is not possible to modify webdynpro setti...

Hi Rodrigo,

Go to the end of the page and select the UIBB which you want the technical configuration and then click on "Configure UIBB" it will take you to the technical configuration

Regards,

Manas

entry.title: Re: No matching records found 'Payment Terms' (OCTG) (ODB...
entry.content:
Hi,

For your issue, check SAP Note 1555242. There is a detect query in the SAP note to confirm the issue is similar or not.

If you are a partner, then you need to log a support ticket to get the issue resolved. If you are a customer, then you need to contact your partner to contact the support via the support ticket.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Replication Cost Center from S4 Hana (S4HCM version)...
entry.content: S/4Hana 2025 release? FYI in S/4Hana our customer version is 2023 version.

entry.title: Re: A/p Down Payment Invoice & A/p Invoice Query
entry.content:
Hi,

You can check the following threads for a start to see if you can adapt something from it or you need to customize your own.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/a-p-invoice-link-to-a-p-downpayment/qaq-p/7137704

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/need-a-report-linking-ar-down-payment-and-ar-invoice-and-ap-down-payment/qaq-p/12215702

You can search for more threads to look if you can find a question where your exact requirement query is already available (with minimum changes to be made)

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: A/p Down Payment Invoice & A/p Invoice Query
Re: Error custom widget on Story
entry.content:
Hi Bernardo Santos, The mostly cause is that the SAC cannot access the Javascript file to render the Custom Image .

Regards

Afshin

entry.title: Stock transfer
entry.content:
I have a scenario here, My Material A qty 56 and amount per unit is 219.04 MAP. So I want to transfer to my new material B amount per unit is 172.75. I want my new material to have my new pricing as MAP 172.75 after posting take place.

I did try using MB1B with mov type 309. The system is copying the original pricing which is 219.04....But i want my new material wt 56qty and MAP 172.75.

Anyone have any idea or solution here. 

 

Thanks

entry.title: ABAP CDS getting count of multiple tables in one CDS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Is it possible to get the count of entries from multiple tables (with no linkage) into a single CDS view.

Actually we are trying to use this CDS for reconciliation purposes and send this information to non SAP data lake to compare the count of records.

Thank You.

Vinod

entry.title: Re: Parameters in data action
entry.content: Actually from your last screenshot that this parameter NOT used yet in the data action, I assume that's why it's not displayed in story through consumption.

entry.title: Re: What to do when there are no free intervals found in...
entry.content:
Hi,

1) First you check option "free intervals" in interval menu, maybe you already checked.
2) Check incremental frequency of the number range and take decision based on that
3) Above two options not work then you need to check about archiving the data, it is last decision you should take after discussion with management.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Sales return orders automatically generate delivery note

entry.title: SAP Integration Content API - Is it possible to view all process direct endpoint value
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I was exploring the available Integration Content API - https://api.sap.com/api/IntegrationContent/overview, 

to view existing data of deployed package/iflows from our SAP CPI. But couldn't find an API which will extract all values of all process direct adapters of deployed artifact / iflows. Is this API feature available?

I have this requirement to extract this data to document all the endpoints to build a detailed landscape of integrations. Since we are using an Async Pipepline, and uses an interface routing iflow which points the received idoc to multiple process direct destination. Need to identify the which iflow the process direct endpoint is used.

@

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @Atsushi_Ohori ,

It is in one of sub page of this topic, general display name can be configured via associated name format. Specifically you can refer to below page  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/70097a1a469d47a0ae08809e4a240f98/0dc9b1490b9b45f89bbe1c0300ef32da.html

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: Sales return orders automatically generate delivery note
entry.content:
Hi @Selina-Wu 

答案部分的貼圖就是針對您提的這個點，您可以在系統中按操作指導一測便知。

entry.title: Re: Disabling the Item Selection Check-boxes of List Outp...
entry.content:
@junwuthanks a lot! for the precise point you highlighted here.

Had a check with the Fiori Team, and there was a small miss because of which this was not working,

Instead of "selectionMode" we had got it maintained as "selectionmode" , we got this corrected and now it works fine 🙂

For anyone looking for something similar to this,

"Page Map" feature which is part of the "SAP Fiori Tools" is the answer!

Some helpful links:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIORI_tools/17d50220bcd848aa854c9c182d65b699/2d8b1cb11f6541e5ab16f05461c64201.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIORI_tools/17d50220bcd848aa854c9c182d65b699/bae38e6216754a76896b926a3d6ac3a9.html

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/fiori-tools-rap-modify-object-page..html

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @SebastianGr1 ,

I think what you are referring is the SAP HCM add-on which works for hybrid mode of SuccessFactors. It is not in SF scope, but would you share how the displayName is managed in SAP HCM and mapped with SF EP field? We can forward this request to SAP HCM. 

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: IAS Deletes Business User Group in SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

sometimes there is also a .roles[] section with the "Users" part of the transformation.
Maybe that's coming in empty and is mapping to the business groups...

But that would be depending on the target system type...
Maybe there's something about the business groups in the corresponding target system documentation

regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP HR TRANSACTION CODES
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Parameters in data action
entry.content: Yes, I can confirm parameters that are not used are not shown during Starter Configuration as a selection is irrelevant to Data Action execution.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS getting count of multiple tables in one CDS
entry.content: You can do that with AMDP easily. If you must have a CDS interface, you can prepare the CDS in the form of a table function and write AMDP behind it.

entry.title: Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update
entry.content:
Just recently, Joule - our generative AI copilot turned 1!
In this one year, Joule has revolutionized the way how users interact with their business solutions, be it HR, Sales, Procurement or IT – but there was one significant piece missing: mobile access.

With our latest releases of SAP Mobile Start, you can now use Joule while being mobile. Instead of searching for the right app, opening it, and then looking for the info, you can now ask Joule in natural language.

While our teams add more and more business content/ scenarios and functions to Joule, we also plan to embed Joule into further mobile apps, like SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Service and Asset Manager.

But now, let´s have a look at how Joule within SAP Mobile Start helps you work faster, get smarter insights when you need them, and interact more smoothly with your business systems while being mobile.

The Joule mobile client is embedded within the SAP Mobile Start header bar and can be opened with a single tap from any main screen. It provides the same main functionality and content as the desktop client if the content is enabled for mobile usage.

Screenshot: SAP Mobile Start with embedded Joule

After opening the Joule client, you just enter your commands in natural language and Joule provides you with the respective answer.

Let´s have a look at three concrete examples:
Imagine you are in a meeting and need to check on your sales orders that have not been delivered so far. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just ask “Show sales orders with delivery status not completed” and Joule will provide you the respective list.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example open orders (SAP S/4HANA)

Or you are a consultant who is new to the role, and a customer asks you how to customize a sales order in SAP S/4HANA. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just pull out your phone and ask Joule, and you will get immediately the right SAP Help pages listed.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example documentation (SAP Help)

Finally, imagine you have a new team member, Maria, who has just joined from another team, and you want to quickly check when she has initially joined the company. Just ask Joule.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example hiring date (SAP SuccessFactors)

 

Joule also allows you to navigate to other apps to get further details. Those could be web apps or native apps like the native SAP SuccessFactors app. So, if you want to learn more about Maria and her career so far, you just tap the “Profile” button and Joule will open Maria´s profile in the native SAP SuccessFactors app on your phone, with all the information about your new team member.

 Screenshot: Example native app integration

To initiate a Joule conversation, you're not limited to type in the request. You can now use Siri and Siri Shortcuts to seamlessly interact with Joule - which even works better with Apple Intelligence. Simply say "Hey Siri, ask SAP. Show my sales orders with delivery status not completed" and Joule will provide you with the respective content within the app.

Joule truly changes the way you interact with your business systems – it becomes much more natural. You can articulate your business needs in natural language and Joule responds by swiftly providing seamless access to your enablement content what will help you to execute your tasks faster and in a far more convenient way.

As of today, not all your prompts and web apps are supported on the phone; but more and more Joule business skills and scenarios are added. We are also working on adding some valuable mobile-specific Joule capabilities, so stay tuned for more.

If you have enabled Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, or SAP Start, the mobile Joule client will automatically be available in SAP Mobile Start. No additional steps are needed.

Do not miss our brand new Joule in SAP Mobile Start video: “Bring Joule Everywhere! SAP's AI Copilot is Now Available on Mobile”. 

Besides bringing Joule to mobile, we have added more helpful features to further enhance your experience with SAP Mobile Start, like Personalization of the Start Screen. With the new release, you can easily move the Start Screen sections such as Insights or News up or down as they work best for you – and hide sections if you do not need them. As always, it is super easy and intuitive and will boost your productivity as you can adjust the Start Screen now, based on your preferences.

 Screenshot: SAP Start Screen personalization

Finally, we have enhanced the Apps and To-Dos Screen. Now you can expand and collapse your spaces and pages section on the app screen, and we have provided an enhanced filtering experience on the To-Do screen that allows you to set multiple filters.

Screenshot: To-Dos Screen with filtering option

I hope you will enjoy those new features in SAP Mobile Start.

For further information on the new topics, please check our SAP Mobile Start documentation.
SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

entry.title: Re: Update ACDOCP table from ABAP options
entry.content:
Hi @Susanne_Helbig ,

I have checked the API as well as the implementation of the app Import Financial Plan Data. So, I am trying to use the method "post_plan_data" of the interface if_fins_planning_api_write with all necessary inputs. Once I execute I am getting 'Plan data posted' success message but the records are not getting updated in the ACDOCP table. I am not sure if I am missing some information or proceeding in the right path.

Please let me know if you can guide me here.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: To check absence exist
entry.content: Hello, Did anyone successfully implement this scenario?

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS getting count of multiple tables in one CDS
entry.content: What did you try? Depends on your context, ABAP release, RAP, etc. Possibilities that you may explore: "IMPLEMENTED BY METHOD" with AMDP class, annotation @ObjectModel.query.implementedBy, or the evil one CDS view per "table" doing a COUNT(*) plus one CDS view with cross join of these "count" CDS views to sum the totals.

entry.title: How to Integrate WebSocket with sap commerce for bi-directional communication
entry.content:
We have to integrate WebSocket in SAP commerce to enable bi-directional communication like push notification. 


our client will be AWS server, and we need to push messages from SAP commerce end.


could you please help on how we can achieve this.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Integrate WebSocket with sap commerce for bi-d...
entry.content:
1, SAP side: using Spring WebSocket to implement WebSocketHandler to handle message and using java.net.http.WebSocket for sending message.
2, AWS side: using AWS API Gateway to configure WebSocket API and using SQS or SNS for bi-directional communication.

entry.title: No BAPI/Functionality available for Passing Inspection Characteristics to Maintenance Tasklist
entry.content: Is there any BAPI/FM available to pass inspection characteristics to the Maintenance Tasklist in sap.

entry.title: Re: Shared Folder Path in Automatic Creation of Sales Orders from Excel (4G5)

Hi Experts,

Could anyone Provide an update here?

Thanks,
Anirban

entry.title: Re: Fiori Launchpad - Deep link to Standard App
Re: aFacetValueIds is Null error in SAC transport
entry.content:
Hi Pranav Vyas,

I got the information from SAP Support, that there will be a Bugfix on the weekend from 14th to 15th of December. Fingers crossed 🙂

Regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: Expose external API to SAP DM
entry.content: Issue seems to be in Manage Web Servers. Create Web Server with type OnPrem instead of Cloud Services, and add the relevant Cloud Connector.

entry.title: Error when create Actions in Process Automation with SAP Business One Service Layer
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am creating an Actions to call Services Layer objects from Process Automation but couldn't create Actions.

I've created Destinations in BTP but when create Actions in SAP Builds errors happen

1. Destinations configuration

2. Create Actions with error

Please help to advise and share your experienced.

Thank you so much,

Best Regards,

Tam

entry.title: Re: Activation of SAP EAM in SAP ECC (not S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Greetings,

that is correct. All core functionalities are out of the box. For some additional functionalities you might need to activate certain business functions in switch framework customizing (SFW5) - some might be subject to licencing.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: RV_SALES_DOCUMENT_VIEW_3 - Customer selection not working
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I'm facing an issue with function module RV_SALES_DOCUMENT_VIEW_3 when trying to obtain open sales orders, similar to transaction VA05.

Adding either material or sales order values in VBCOM provides the necessary filtered output. 
However, the filtering doesn't seem to be working by customer number (KUNNR).

Do you have any insights on how this issue can be overcome? I want to filter out open sales orders at a CUSTOMER LEVEL.
Any suggestions welcome.

Best Regards,
Samith

entry.content:

We are encountering an issue while syncing data with the standard metadata of SAM 2405.

Below are the screenshots of the trace during the syncing process, along with the error log:

 @bill_froelich, @Jitendra_Kansal I appreciate any help you can provide.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Denmark Localisation for SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hi Miguel,

you can find the latest statement from SAP on the registered system topic in Note 3338991 - DK_ERP System Registration with Authorities - SAP for Me

In Referenced Document section of the Note you can find the changes SAP has done for Denmark localization to make it ready like SAF-T export, status of OIOUBL format support etc. Up to my knowledge Version FP 2402 and above are ready for the minimum requirements which start January 2025.

 It is possible to operate systems that are not on the officially registered but it is then your responsibility to ensure that you fullfill all the requirements of Bookkeeping Act. These are system topics but also process topics (documented processes, backups, bank reconciliation). There are partnes in Denmark who also sell you the whole bundle.

entry.title: Re: Hide custom fields in position object template
entry.content:
Hi,

You can either set the visibility of the field to Not visible or not include the field in Manage Configuration UI. Furthermore, you have the possibility to set the visibility based on UI Rules.

entry.title: Re: Impossible to view a document via CMC
entry.content: Thanks, I'll try this !

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content:
Greetings,

you can follow the procedure as described in the following blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/in-process-inspection-in-production-at-regular-intervals/ba-p/13249033

Essentially you need to properly define master data (sampling procedure, inspection plan or routing) and then create inspection points manually or automatically if the scenario supports it. 

The following fields are not available in QP* transactions.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Export Configuration CBC
entry.content: Hello Sven, do you have a date planned for the release of this functionality? Thanks and best regards, Annika

entry.title: Re: Behavior of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation after Clearing
entry.content:
Dear @Ces621 

Thank you for your feedback and showing interest in Advanced Valuation.

The reason is, that Advanced Valuation is not integrated into clearing process. E.g. in scenario 4, the incoming payment calculates and posts a FX valuation (FX valuation gain) of 200 EUR. Since the FX valuation gain of 100 EUR in step 2 is not considered, step 3 (incoming payment) need to be corrected by step 4 with -100 EUR by the last Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation run. 

This shows, that is required to run Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation even though the clearing happened. 

In case of further questions, please feel free to reach out or comment. 

Appreciate your comment and question. 

Thank you, 

Kevin

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content: Hello, and if I use inspection type 04

entry.title: Re: Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA, Private Cloud Edition – Essential Knowledge Base, Lear
entry.content: Just to mention - the Blog will be kept up to date - kindly comment in case you want me to add valuable information. In that case kindly provide a link to the source you refer to.

entry.title: Sales Order Flexible Workflow (WS02000006)
entry.content:
Hi there,

I am working on a sales order flexible workflow (WS02000006) right now. I have finished all the setup needed to get the workflow going, and it is triggering for the creation of a sales order for the specified condition. The workflow is triggerring, and it is sending notification to My Inbox of the defined user having the role to release, reject, or rework the sales order.
But without any manual intervention, SAP_WFRT is automatically releasing the sales order.
How to stop the automatic release of the sales order by SAP_WFRT and assign it to the desired user.

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @HarveyXu ,

yes, I am referring to the SAP HCM add-on and the Talent Hybrid mode. It would be great if you could take this request to the SAP HCM team.

A great first step would be that the set of supported fields of the SAP HCM add-on is enhanced with the displayName of SuccessFactors, so that customers could at least write their own extraction method and deliver the display name. Here you can find the list of supported fields: Mapping of SuccessFactors Fields for Employee Data to SAP ERP | SAP Help Portal

In SAP HCM you define name formats, similar to SuccessFactors. The formated employee name is then typically stored in the field ENAME of infotype 0002. Personal Data (Infotype 0002) | SAP Help Portal

Thanks and regards
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: Need information on migrating from SAML protocol to OIDC
entry.content:
I guess with the switch you'll have to create a new "Custom Identity Provider for Applications" as you can't just switch the protocol. With that I guess you'll loose the user - role-collection assignments as the users are created for dedicated IDPs... 

Beside that i couldn't find a dedicated relation between UI5 apps and the dedicated Custom Identity Provider for Apps... therefor i guess the App / Role Template / Role / Role Collections structures shouldn't be affected...

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
Re: inspection points time
entry.content:
Greetings,

the functionality is the same but depending on the process your inspection lot will be created at goods receipt if you are not using early inspection lot creation functionality.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Handling Unit Type Check at Putaway aginst storage bin Type
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently using this settings and everything works accordingly regarding the putaway processes. I am facing though issues with internal movements (transfer and posting changes) which are product tasks and the system requires in ADPROD or POST an entry for HU type even though there is a blank entry in the definition of allowed HU type for storage types and bin types. Moreover,  the option to create WT using the "Create WT" from the monitor does not even offer the option to populate this field.

Is there something missing in the configuration of the process so that WT creation outside Inbound process also works?

PS: The current storage types do not permit HU 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content: See the article, it says to use CA02, but in 04 inspection type, we use QP02. If I use CA02 for 04 inspection type, I will need to add characteristics, but why add them if we use QP02. And for 04 inspection type, QP02 is used, I have never seen CA02 used

entry.title: Re: How to automate SAP BTP Platform User management?
entry.content:
You may use the SAP Cloud Identity Services. Each customer gets two tenants for free with his BTP licence. The APIs you mentioned are used by  the SAP Cloud Identity Services. See: SAP Cloud Identity Services | SAP Community

SAP Cloud Identity Services are our central solution for managing authentication, single sign-on, and the identity lifecycle. They improve system integration, provide a seamless user experience, and enhance security and compliance.

SAP Cloud Identity Services consist of the following services:

The Identity Authentication service is a service that provides central capabilities for authentication and single sign-on. The Identity Provisioning service manages identity lifecycle processes for cloud and on-premise systems. The Identity Directory is the persistency layer inside the services. The Authorization Management service allows administrators to assign access based on policies centrally within SAP Cloud Identity Services. An access policy allows a user to perform certain actions on a resource, subject to restricting rules. These rules can be adapted by the administrator so that policies fit company requirements before being assigned to users.

entry.title: Re: Use the Alert Service now across Multiple Plants
entry.content:
Great Blogpost Frank! 

I have thought one question on the notifications configuration in the Alert Type itself. 

I understand that for processors in different Plants now with the new Plant input are restricted to see alerts of different plants. But what happens with the recipients of the notifications set in for example Email Notifications. This field is still not plant specific, ideally would be based on the plant of the user and plant where the alert has been created, that only the email addresses defined as recipient would get the notification. 

Otherwise, we would be still be forced to create plant based alert types, if we want to notify a certain group of people by email.

Hope that was somewhat clear! 

Thanks again!

RG

entry.title: Re: Need some clarification or suggestions regarding T-Co...
entry.content: Hi Dominik, thanks for this but i am looking for any standard in MD07 tcode

entry.title: Re: EWM SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition - Stock valuati...
entry.content:
Hi 

As Hi 

As per my understanding, SAP will dont display the stock valuation of individual storage types and storage bins in EWM SAP S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition. however we can see feasibility with SAP BW  see below It is from EWM so it may give you an idea

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA_CONTENT/06e872f914a44d77b6c692b0273ca400/4801ba1c85784139a32f192ff7497129.html

if you are not using B/W then we need to think another way. in SAP Public Cloud, Sap provides limited to put custom logic and extensibility. Analyze and see possibillty that and check some reports from IM and WM and give logic to display the value. It can display the reports. to give the quantity Quant table and IM level table will help you.

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud Assigning the resources to the proje...
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can assign resources to a project and manage costs by following these steps:

### Assigning Resources to a Project

1. **Access the Resource Management for Projects app:**
- Ensure you have the necessary roles and authorizations, such as the Resource Manager business role (SAP_BR_RESOURCE_MANAGER), to access this app.

2. **Select a Work Package:**
- Within your project, choose the appropriate work package to which you want to assign resources.

3. **Add Team Role:**
- Click on "Add Team Role" to create a new entry for the role to be filled by a resource.
- Select a role and enter the effort and skills required for that role.

4. **Search for Resources:**
- Use the "Add Resource" option to find suitable candidates based on various criteria like name, role, or skills.
- Consider filters such as delivery organization, cost center, and service cost level to refine your search for appropriate resources.

5. **Select and Staff Resources:**
- Once you have identified suitable resources, confirm their staffing. Ensure staffing aligns with their availability for the duration of the work package.

### Managing Costs

1. **Billing Control Category:**
- Decide if the assigned resources are billable or not using the Billing Control Category option during the team role setup.

2. **Monitor Costs:**
- Use cost centers and financial functions within SAP S/4HANA Cloud to track the costs associated with staffing these resources.
- You may utilize the Project Staffing Analysis app to analyze project costs, resources, and effort.

### Additional Notes

- Ensure all relevant master data, such as availability and cost centers, is up to date.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: New SAP Readiness Check Scenario – SAP Readiness Check for SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I am new to DS, and reading up on much information. However, I am yet to find some guides/blogs/notes etc, as what to do with the results from the Readiness check.

Is there anything out there that addresses, the next statges after the Readiness check. What to do with artefacts that need changing, and how to change them to be compatible with DS.

Thanks in advance

g

entry.title: Re: in sap public cloud eppm How to connect CRM with proj...
entry.content:
Hello,

The connection between CRM and projects depends on which CRM you are using.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management (EPPM) module allows for the integration of sales orders with projects. This integration enables you to track and manage project-related sales orders effectively. Here’s a general guide on how to work with sales orders in conjunction with projects:

1. **Project Creation:**
- First, ensure that your project has been created within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud EPPM environment. Make sure that all necessary project details and structures, such as work packages and WBS elements, are in place.

2. **Sales Order Creation:**
- Create a sales order in the system as you normally would. Ensure all details, such as customer details, order items, and terms, are filled in.

3. **Assign Sales Order to Project:**
- During the sales order creation or afterward, you have the ability to assign this order to a specific project. This is typically done by associating order items with relevant project elements, such as Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements.

4. **Use of Public API:**
- If you're integrating data from external sources or systems into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you might use the Public API for project plans and project elements. This allows you to bring in external project information and synchronize it with your cloud setup.

5. **Project Tracking:**
- Once the sales order is linked to the project, you can track and manage the sales order alongside project progress within the EPPM module. This includes monitoring KPIs related to both sales and project execution.

6. **Reporting and Analytics:**
- Utilize the reporting and analytics functions within SAP S/4HANA Cloud to gain insights about the relationship between sales and projects. This can help in understanding project financials, execution states, and sales performance related to projects.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to mass upload the materials (demand) in project...
entry.content:
Hello,

In the current context of the "Manage Project Demand" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, there isn't a direct feature to mass upload material demands from an Excel spreadsheet.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Industry Sector field in Product Master in S4HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Hi 

As you mentioned that you did not find in SAP SAP Public Cloud. I am aware that sap public cloud provides everything limited in terms of configuration and processes. But I would suggest you to check once again in SSCUI if you dont get that then you need check in central business Configuration(CBC) or Configure your solutions

Check SAP help

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/053d7598ef7c49629a6642776c82bdb2.html?&version=2408.00.

While creating product master we have field for industry sector so I think it will be available to confirgure some where. if you dont find then I would suggest you to advise from SAP for it 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: UI5ers Buzz #55: Adaptation Project - your one stop tool for extending SAPUI5 Applications
entry.content:
Hi @hristotsolev,

Thank you for your clarification. We have created an Adaptation Project for My Inbox and called a custom SAPUI5 application to display in the details area. We successfully implemented it, however now we wanted to customize the approval part in which we want to save some data to our custom OData service first before sending it to the workflow. In our older version of My Inbox, we were able to do this by using "sap.ca.scfld". See screenshot below:

However in our new version of My Inbox, we are already encountering an error with this library. Therefore we cannot proceed in sending our requests to the Workflow. Would you know how we can fix this? Or is there any implementation guide you can give as a reference? I couldn't find any article related to it yet. Appreciate much if you could help us with this. Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content:
Greetings,

yes you need to prepare different master data for 04 inspection. The preparation is very similar. There might be a problem since in inspection plan the following fields are not available.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: BAPI for Material storage location wise stock check
entry.content:
You can try calling BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY and then reading MARD directly from a function module such as MARD_ARRAY_READ or RFC_READ_TABLE.

(But feel free to search your system for a mapping FM with a name such as MAP2E_MARD_TO_BAPI*, then locate the corresponding BAPI, usually in the same function group or referenced with the use case).

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content: In my opinion, just for the 04 inspection type, we use the QP transaction, because in the header we have a different use of 05, not 01. If we use 01, there will be errors

entry.title: I would like to disable verify course completion button
entry.content: Is it possible to disable verify course completion button? It is very confusing and since the customer is using "AICC wrapper" there is really no point in having that.

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content: I don't have a window like you have in the screenshot. Is the screenshot taken from the CA02 transaction?

entry.title: Re: SAP Code-Jam Request in Dubai

--
Warm Regards,
Harish Vatsa

entry.title: Re: Field "Ship-to Party's Customer Reference Date" in SD...
entry.content:
Hello,

You may need to involve custom logic if you find out that it's not directly available in your standard data sources or forms.

1. **Data Source Extension**:
- If you’ve already extended the data source (FDP_V1_ORDER_CONFIRM_SRV) and couldn’t find VBKD-BSTDK_E, it's possible that the field is not exposed in the standard SAP S/4HANA Cloud services. Extending data sources is usually the first step, but if that doesn’t work, the Custom Logic approach would indeed be required.

2. **Scouting Fields in Forms by Technical Name**:
- Generally, finding a field in Public Cloud forms using only the technical name isn’t straightforward. You would typically do the following:
- **Enhanced Developer Extensibility**: Use this to add additional fields if available.
- **Form Template Customization**: Modify the form template in the Form Builder and attempt to add the field manually. Testing directly on the Public Cloud as you mentioned is often a practical approach.
- **Custom Logic (ABAP in SAP S/4HANA Cloud)**: If the field isn't available, you might consider using an enhancement logic to populate or fetch the required field data.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: User Acceptance Testing
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: User Acceptance Testing
entry.content: Hi The below link contains all the information you need with regards to testing https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/types-of-testing-terminologies-asap/ba-p/13250463

entry.content:
Greetings,

that is correct yes. The screenshot was taken from CA* transaction. The fields are not available in QP* transactions.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Creating Split Item Object

entry.title: Crystal Reports SDK Intergated Security not working with Oracle
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm using the .NET Crystal Reports SDK to handle reports in our system. Although the Integrated Security flag is set to True in the code, I still get prompted to provide a username and password. Checking "Integrated Security" in the prompt works as expected, and the report is processed. Is there any known issue related to this?
I have tested that on the newest sdk version

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content: Can we use time points for type 04 verification in QP02? Tell me, please, have you tried the method described on other inspection type, for example 04?

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What' s New in Q4 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q4 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance we have added new product pages.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add three new product pages that consolidate links to all available release communication channels, like schedules, pre- and post-release communication, blogs, and more.

The new pages are:

  1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  2. SAP Mobile Services
  3. SAP Translation Hub

Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, documentation on releases and available modules, the GitHub Kyma repositories, as well as a helpful learning journey and hands-on tutorials.

Check it out [Add link].

Fig 1. The new page for SAP BTP, Kyma runtime.

 

2. SAP Mobile Services

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out [Add link].

Fig 2. The new page for SAP Mobile Services.

 

3. SAP Translation Hub

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out [Add link].

Fig 3. The new page for SAP Translation Hub.

 

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 30 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

 

Please note that the product page for SAP Data Intelligence Cloud has been retired as of this update. For more information, you can refer to this blog post.

 

For centralized information on when SAP BTP products and services release new features refer to the Consolidated Release Schedule for SAP BTP.

Regarding Release Navigator updates, you can refer to this update plan:

*Disclaimer:  The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.

Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

 

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

 

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Re: inspection points time
entry.content: Greetings, you can use inspection points in 04 inspection type BUT unfortunately you cannot define the interval of inspection since 04 inspction is primarily inetended for inspection at goods receipt. Even if you use 04 with early inspection lot creation you cannot define the interval.
